content: Skip to login   Skip to main content   Skip to chat     Business     EFTPOS, payments and merchants     Merchant support centre     Enhanced functions on EFTPOS terminal   Enhanced functions on EFTPOS terminal   Everything to know on setting up and making use of enhanced functions on your eftpos terminal.    Taking payments via phone on EFTPOS terminal Ingenico    To take phone transactions/payments you'll need to have manual key entry function setup. To setup this function, you'll need to apply by calling our Merchant Service Centre on 1300 369 852 available 24/7. Make sure you have your Merchant ID and Terminal ID handy before you call.    You can also see if you have this function in the pricing schedule found in your letter of offer.    We recommend you don't manually enter card details without first swiping or inserting the card in the card readers. You'll need your customer's signature if you choose this function. You'll also be charged a fee for this type of transaction.     Press Menu.  Press 1 to select Transaction.  Press 7 to select MOTO.  Enter your sale amount and press Enter.  Enter your customer's card number and press Enter.  Enter the card expiry date in MMYY format and press Enter.  Enter CCV number from the back of the card or press Enter to select appropriate option.  Select MOTO Type (1. Telephone Order. 2. Mail Order. 3. Internet Order).  Select MOTO Transaction Frequency/Type (1. Single, 2. Recurring and 3. Instalment).  Press Enter to confirm the transaction.  Once the approval displays one your screen your receipt will print.  Press Yes to print your customer their receipt.    Important: If you need to regularly use the manual key entry function, you should consider our NAB Transaction MOTO product. You can chat with our sales team to find out more on 1300 888 767.    Void the last transaction on an EFTPOS terminal Ingenico     This function allows the last performed transaction to be cancelled and is an optional feature that can be enabled/disabled by calling Merchant Services. A void transaction will not appear on the Merchant Statement, Settlement report or cardholder's statement.  Press the Menu button on the home screen.  Select 3 for Batch then 5 for Void Last.  Review the transaction details and press Enter to confirm.  The merchant copy of the voided receipt will print automatically.    Processing a cash out transaction on an EFTPOS terminal Ingenico    The cash out feature is available on debit (cheque and savings) accounts only for EFTPOS transactions. It is an optional feature that you may enable or disable by contacting the Merchant Service Centre.     Press the Menu button on the home screen.  Press 1 to select Transaction.  Press 3 to select Cash Out. Enter the required cash out amount and press Enter.  Prompt the customer to swipe or insert their card.  Prompt the customer to select the account. Only CHQ or SAV can be chosen.  Prompt the customer to enter their PIN and press Enter.  An acknowledgement will be displayed once the cash out has been approved.  Remove the card from the terminal and provide the customer with their card.  Press Yes if the customer requires a receipt.    Important information    This section contains Important Information relevant to the page you are viewing, but you can't see it because you have JavaScript disabled on your browser. Please enable JavaScript and come back so you can see the complete page. It's important that you read the Important Information in this section before acting on any information on this page.
